mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals labor market expansion and rising aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rolling 3-year Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03, or if the mean return after positive PAYEMS shocks is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, it indicates labor market weakness and rising recession risk. Elevated joblessness depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If in more than 60% of months with UNRATE above 5.5% the subsequent 60-day SPX return is positive, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls for two consecutive months signal a broad economic contraction. Falling output and employment erode corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If during such contractionary signals, the 20-day SPX return is positive more than 40% of the time, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles when the federal funds rate is above 3% and rising, further increases in the rate compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions, resulting in negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If during tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 3% and trending upward) a further increase in FEDFUNDS is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than half of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer spending and overall economic momentum. Strong demand supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward SPX return is negatively correlated with RSAFS growth over any 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.03), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) is the definitive signal of economic recession. Falling aggregate output drastically reduces corporate earnings and investor confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If after a negative GDP print the subsequent 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and the federal funds rate indicate that rising mortgage costs are not yet cooling construction, an unsustainable dynamic that prefigures a downturn in housing and the broader economy, punishing equity prices over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and FEDFUNDS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If when both HOUST and FEDFUNDS rise together the 20-day SPX return is positive more often than negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment environment (UNRATE below 5%), rising industrial production signals robust supply expansion without excessive labor slack, boosting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If when unemployment is below 5%, rising INDPRO is associated with negative or zero 20-day forward SPX returns in more than 50% of months, reject.
